content.problemStatement:
Chef found NNN magical cards in his drawer. Each card has a number written on each of its faces. He places all the cards on the table in a front face-up manner.

Chef denotes the numbers on the front face by A1,A2,...,ANA_1, A_2,..., A_NA1​,A2​,...,AN​ and on the back face by B1,B2,...,BNB_1, B_2,..., B_NB1​,B2​,...,BN​. Chef can choose some (possibly zero or all) cards and flip them, then he will calculate the bitwise AND of all the numbers currently in a face-up manner.

Now Chef wonders what is the maximum bitwise AND that he can achieve and what is the minimum number of cards he has to flip to achieve this value. Can you help him find it?


Input Format

The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
Each test case contains three lines of input.
The first line contains a single integer NNN - the number of cards.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,...,ANA_1, A_2,..., A_NA1​,A2​,...,AN​ - the numbers on the front face of the cards
The third line contains NNN space-separated integers B1,B2,...,BNB_1, B_2,..., B_NB1​,B2​,...,BN​ - the numbers on the back face of the cards


Output Format
For each test case, print a single line containing two space-separated integers, first denoting the maximum bitwise AND possible and second denoting the minimum number of flips required to achieve it.
constraints: Sum of N over all test cases ≤ 10^5
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case: the first line contains an integer N, the number of cards. The second line contains N space‑separated integers A1 … AN – the numbers on the front faces. The third line contains N space‑separated integers B1 … BN – the numbers on the back faces.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line with two integers separated by a space: the maximum possible bitwise AND of the visible numbers after any set of flips, and the minimum number of flips required to obtain that maximum.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: The maximum AND is obtained after flipping the first and third cards achieving a configuration of [2,6,2][2, 6, 2][2,6,2] which yields a bitwise AND of 222.
Test case 222: Every possible configuration of the cards yields a bitwise AND equal to 000. So to ensure the minimum number of operations, Chef performs no flip.

solution.approach: For a candidate value X we can achieve it iff for every card i at least one of the two numbers (Ai or Bi) contains all bits of X, i.e. (Ai & X) == X or (Bi & X) == X. Starting with answer = 0 we try to set bits from the most significant (30) down to 0. For each bit we form a temporary candidate = answer | (1<<bit) and test the feasibility condition above in O(N). If feasible we keep the bit, otherwise we discard it. After the loop answer holds the maximum possible AND. To obtain the minimum number of flips we simply count the cards where the front face does not satisfy the final answer but the back face does – those cards must be flipped; if the front already satisfies the answer we keep it to avoid unnecessary flips. The algorithm runs in O(N·B) per test case where B = 31 (the number of bits), well within limits.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N · 31) per test case, i.e. O(N) overall because the number of bits is constant.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) extra besides the input arrays.
